name: Injera Bread (Ethiopian)
recipeInstructions:
note - Jeff says that Gold Medal works the best!!
Put the warm water into a blender or food processor. Add the flour to the water and blend, starting slowly and then increasing speed. You should probably do this in two batches unless you have a powerful blender. Scrape down the sides with a rubber spatula from both the blender and processor. Place the batter in a 6 cup bowl and stir in the club soda. Heat a 12 inch coated pan to 400 degrees (Jeff recommends an electric skillet but I don't have one!). Using a ladle, pour 1/4 cup of batter onto one corner of the hot pan. Immediately tilt the pan so that the batter covers the bottom evenly. Cook, uncovered, until the top is filled with holes and no longer wet. The edges should begin to curl just slightly. Remove quickly with your fingers and place the bread on a kitchen towel. Let it cool for 3-4 minutes as you prepare the next bread in a similar manner, and continue until all batter is used. Place the cooled bread on a plate. Stack the cooled breads on top of each other and cover with plastic wrap until ready to serve. Can be made 3 hours prior to serving.
